title: Truss Lab — Bridge Architect
description: Build a real truss bridge with wood, steel & tension-only cable, then stress-test it with a truck.
concept: A physics-toy bridge builder: place joints and wire them into a truss with three materials of differing cost, strength and stiffness, then send a truck across. A mass-spring simulation computes real tension/compression per beam, color-codes stress live, and snaps members that exceed their material's limit. Three levels raise the span, budget and truck weight, and every design can be saved, shared or screenshotted.
features: 3 materials — wood, steel, and a tension-only cable that goes slack under compression — each with real cost-per-length and a force limit
Live mass-spring physics: beams sag and stress-color from green to red under load, and snap if overloaded
3 levels of increasing span, budget and truck weight, each needing a genuinely triangulated truss to survive
Save/share designs via JSON export-import, a shareable URL hash, and a PNG screenshot export, plus a localStorage best-cost record per level
tech: Mass-spring-damper physics with semi-implicit Euler substeps

interaction.mouse: Click empty space to place a joint; switch to Beam tool and click two joints to connect them with the selected material
interaction.click: Toolbar switches between Joint / Beam / Erase tools and Wood / Steel / Cable materials
interaction.touch: Same as mouse — tap to place joints and connect beams
